passage: The following text is from Joan Didion’s memoir The Year of Magical Thinking. In the text, the author discusses her home life.
stem:
[I]n California we heated our houses by building fires. We built fires even on summer evenings, because the fog came in. _Fires said we were home, we had drawn the circle, we were safe through the night._

Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?
choices: It illustrates that a fire provides comfort beyond physical warmth.
It summarizes the information that came before it in the text.
It explains that the house remains cold even in summer.
It suggests that the author feels comfortable in her home with or without a fire.
answer: It illustrates that a fire provides comfort beyond physical warmth.
explanation: The first sentences explain that fires heated the house year-round; the underlined sentence adds that the fires also meant being “home” and feeling “safe,” an emotional comfort beyond warmth. It doesn’t summarize the earlier lines, claim the house stays cold, or suggest the author feels equally at ease without a fire.
